evals.prompt: My analysis notebook pulls monthly sales data, cleans it, computes KPI aggregates, and generates charts for a report. Management wants this delivered automatically every Monday morning. It's 800 lines of cells with hardcoded dates and file paths. How do I productionize it?
evals.expected_output: Should refactor incrementally: extract the logic into importable modules, parameterize the hardcoded dates/paths, add tests for the transformations, pin the environment for reproducibility, schedule the Monday run with failure alerting — not rewrite from scratch.
assertions: Extracts notebook logic into importable modules/functions rather than leaving everything in cells
Parameterizes the hardcoded dates and paths (config file, CLI args, or papermill-style notebook parameters)
Adds tests for the extracted transformation logic
Pins the environment for reproducibility (lock file, pinned requirements, or a container)
Sets up scheduling for the Monday run sized to the team (cron or CI schedule before reaching for a heavy orchestrator)
Addresses failure handling and alerting for the unattended run
Proposes an incremental refactor path rather than a from-scratch rewrite

evals.prompt: Our team has 30+ research notebooks accumulated over two years. Some are one-off explorations, some get re-run every quarter by hand. We can't productionize everything — what should we do?
evals.expected_output: Should triage rather than migrate everything: productionize the recurring high-impact notebooks first, archive one-offs with enough context to be reproducible, extract shared utilities for duplicated logic, and set a lightweight standard for future notebooks.
assertions: Triages the notebooks instead of proposing to productionize all 30+ — one-off explorations stay notebooks
Prioritizes by re-run frequency and business impact (the quarterly hand-run ones first)
Recommends archiving one-offs with enough context to reproduce them later (data snapshot or query, pinned environment)
Proposes extracting shared utilities for logic duplicated across notebooks
Suggests a lightweight convention for future notebooks that are likely to need productionizing
Does not propose a big-bang migration of everything

evals.prompt: I have a notebook that does feature engineering on our user events and trains a ranking model. I want to turn it into a pipeline that retrains automatically every week.
evals.expected_output: Should recognize this notebook feeds model TRAINING — feature engineering, training, evaluation, scheduled retraining — and hand off to the ml-pipeline-design skill for the training-pipeline DAG, rather than doing a general notebook-to-module refactor.
assertions: Recognizes the notebook feeds model training and hands off to or invokes the ml-pipeline-design skill
Names training-pipeline concerns (pipeline DAG stages, data validation, evaluation gate, retraining schedule) as ml-pipeline-design territory
Does not respond with only a generic notebook-to-module refactor as the primary answer
